_note: Original Practice questions aligned with CS Navigator's original Learn lesson for this topic.

questions.title: Classes & Objects practice 1
questions.prompt: What is the main job of a constructor?
questions.explanation: The constructor initializes the new object so its public operations can rely on a valid state.

questions.title: Classes & Objects practice 2
choices: It promises not to modify the object through that method
It makes the returned integer constant forever
questions.explanation: The trailing const lets callers and the compiler know that reading the balance does not change the account.

questions.title: Trace Classes & Objects
questions.prompt: What output does this code produce? Enter each line in order.
questions.explanation: The constructor initializes the balance, deposit changes it through a rule, and the const getter reads it without modifying the object. The resulting output is '25'.

questions.title: Classes & Objects practice 5
questions.prompt: In this class, why is `balance_` under `private` while `deposit` and `balance` are `public`?
choices: So the balance can only change through methods that enforce the class's rules
So the balance uses less memory than a public field
So the balance is shared across every Account object
questions.explanation: A private field cannot be assigned from outside the class, so no caller can write `account.balance_ = -50`. Every change has to go through `deposit`, which is the one place that can reject an invalid amount. Access level is about who may change the data, not about memory, sharing, or compile speed.

questions.title: Classes & Objects concept check 1
questions.prompt: What does the lesson mean by "Initialize every member"?
choices: A constructor body runs after data members have already been initialized. Prefer the member initializer list for their starting values, especially for const members and objects that have no default constructor.
questions.explanation: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. That reasoning supports the answer: A constructor body runs after data members have already been initialized. Prefer the member initializer list for their starting values, especially for const members and objects that have no default constructor.

questions.title: Classes & Objects concept check 2
questions.prompt: Which choice describes a reliable Classes & Objects practice?
choices: It makes the returned integer constant forever
Give the class one clear responsibility.
questions.explanation: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. That reasoning supports the answer: Give the class one clear responsibility.

questions.title: Classes & Objects concept check 3
questions.prompt: Which recommendation belongs in a review of Classes & Objects code?
choices: It makes the returned integer constant forever
Initialize every member in the constructor.
questions.explanation: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. That reasoning supports the answer: Initialize every member in the constructor.

questions.title: Classes & Objects concept check 4
questions.prompt: Which guideline helps prevent a common Classes & Objects mistake?
questions.explanation: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. That reasoning supports the answer: Keep invariants behind private data.

questions.title: Classes & Objects concept check 5
questions.prompt: A classmate is checking their understanding of Classes & Objects. Which advice is correct?
questions.explanation: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. That reasoning supports the answer: Mark read-only methods const.

questions.title: Classes & Objects concept check 6
questions.prompt: What is the main idea behind Classes & Objects?
choices: Design types that keep related state and behavior together.
questions.explanation: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. That reasoning supports the answer: Design types that keep related state and behavior together.
